description: A conceptual diagram of THz time-domain spectroscopy (THz-TDS) data processing showing a measured electric field signal E(x0, y0, t) in the time domain, its transformation into the frequency domain via FFT as E(f) = A(f) exp[iφ(f)] with amplitude and phase components, and reconstruction of a single-frequency time-domain oscillation using inverse FFT.
key_findings: The FFT decomposes a time-domain THz signal into frequency-dependent amplitude and phase components.
A single frequency component can be isolated and reconstructed back into a sinusoidal time-domain signal using inverse FFT.
suggested_knowledge_points: Relationship between time-domain and frequency-domain representations of signals
Interpretation of amplitude and phase spectra
Use of FFT and inverse FFT in spectroscopy
